author: anon-a7c6025f
content:
Observation: cudarc provides CudaContext::alloc_pinned<T>() → PinnedHostSlice<T> and clone_htod/memcpy_htod accept PinnedHostSlice via the HostSlice trait. The CUDA driver automatically uses DMA for pinned→device transfers.

Design:
1. Add pin_memory() method to GpuBackend trait — allocates pinned host memory and copies CPU data into it
2. Add PinnedBuffer wrapper in ferrotorch-gpu that holds a PinnedHostSlice
3. Add pin_memory option to DataLoader that converts batch tensors to pinned storage
4. Tensor::pin_memory() method that creates a CPU tensor backed by pinned memory
5. When .to(device) is called on a pinned tensor, use the fast DMA path
